reasoning: The USDA FSIS takes a surprisingly long time to mark recalls as "Closed." For a recall initiated on April 24, 2025, a closure before July 1, 2025 gives a window of exactly 67 days. Based on historical FSIS recall data, the average time to formally close a recall is typically well over 100 days. Although one example (King's Command Foods, misbranding) closed in just 59 days, numerous other recent misbranding and undeclared allergen recalls took between 120 and 200+ days (e.g., Aunt Kitty's Foods took 120 days, Green Valley Foods took 201 days) even for relatively small amounts of product (<5,000 lbs). The ACC Central Kitchen recall is massive in comparison (nearly 65,000 lbs), which likely requires substantially more time for the firm to conduct retrievals and for the FSIS Recall Management and Technical Analysis Division to verify the effectiveness checks and complete the administrative closure process. Therefore, the probability of this large recall closing within the very tight 67-day window is estimated to be quite low (12%).
